head: claude/the-field-reframe
note: hand merge by the owner via the GitHub button, 2026-06-12T18:10:19Z; receipt reconciled by the session so the digest is not blind to a real land

reason: Value is the owner's, on the record: bdo confirmed epic.the-field today (adm.6e3ccedc1f0b), and the arc's own text names the questions this fold answers - where work is, what level, whose authority, what is safe. The story commits to the disciplines that earned the arc its reframe: a pure read-only fold (gaps.py is the proof-of-shape it generalises), absent rungs surfaced as named gaps rather than invented (absence is information), and occupancy rendering authority (an occupant with no realness admission shows un-authorised - exactly the failure mode done-line 0049 caught live). Downstream declared (seam.handoff-to-confirm) - the omission handoff sent v2 of gates-enumerated back for. The Section-10 check that could have failed and did not: the story could have claimed task/environment/occupant as buildable first-class rungs today - that fiction would have been reject_wrong_value; it instead names them partial and surfaces them as gaps. Hesitation on the record: this verdict comes from the summoned session through the one pen after two headless runs of this same gate timed out at the 600s cap (issues 95, 96) - same class, same rung (adm.6ce59640e214), same prompt-hash; the run-seam timeout is an infra fault to repair, not a reason to leave the seam jammed.
arc epic.the-field confirmed by bdo (adm.6e3ccedc1f0b) — the owner's standing stamp

Deterministic placement law (loop.placement_gate, read-only preview run 2026-06-12): atom.field-topology.v0 places cleanly - its one touched address (loop/field.py) is new and overlaps no sibling's declared off-limits set; must_not_collide_with is empty and no sibling names it. The check that could have failed: a sibling declaring loop/field.py off-limits, the collision the probe atom proved this gate refuses (rcp history of placement-gate.det.v1).

Deterministic handoff law (loop.handoff_gate, read-only preview run 2026-06-12): the atom carries a story, a build surface (loop/field.py) and a declared downstream (seam.handoff-to-confirm) - the three things an implementer needs to start without guessing. The check that could have failed and did for its arc-sibling: a missing hands_off_to, exactly what this gate sent gates-enumerated.v2 back for (rcp.2b1680fc9075).

The story claims a delivered artifact: 'a deterministic read-only fold that, given one arc, maps its work-topology', incidence touches loop/field.py. Measured against the tree and the record: loop/field.py does NOT exist (loop/ holds census, digest, gaps, handoff_gate, merge, minds, node, orchestrate, pen, placement_gate, reconcile, reflect, runs, summon, tags, trust, web — no field). The log shows this atom passing every earlier gate — value accept (rcp.460a2559520e), owner-stamp accept (rcp.2605d15b83a8), placement sound (rcp.fd5cad4fb49e), handoff ready_for_spec (rcp.007c4d638d11) — but NO merge receipt, no atom.landed, no delivery event: each earlier gate judged the STORY and its PLACEMENT, all sound, and none could see that the build never happened. That is the claim/delivery gap this gate exists to catch (§10): the one piece of delivery evidence that could have been absent and is — loop/field.py itself, the fold the story promised. This is not a re-judgment of value (L0's accept stands; the Field is worth building); it is the honest report that intention and outcome diverged — ready_for_spec was reached, the spec was never built. The move: a session builds loop/field.py against done-line 0050 and lands it, then re-announces. missed.

Value is the owner's, on the record: bdo confirmed epic.the-field himself (adm.6e3ccedc1f0b), and this atom is that arc's first piece - the decomposition ladder the arc's horizon names verbatim. The story's evidence verified against reality, not taken on its word: loop/field.py and tests/test_field.py exist on disk; rcp.merge.101 is on receipts.jsonl (merge-node.claude.v1 landed PR 101, head claude/field-fold, authorized_by adm.6e3ccedc1f0b, 20:10:54Z); the missed it answers, rcp.3e08d1446381 (value-confirm.claude.v1 on v0, 19:58:13Z), says exactly what the story says it says - every earlier gate passed the story while loop/field.py did not exist, ready_for_spec reached, spec never built, move named: build against done-line 0050, land, re-announce. That move is done and the land post-dates the missed by twelve minutes. The claimed disciplines are in the delivered code, not just the story: task/environment render as named gaps with needs-bdo moves (absence is information), and occupancy carries authority - an occupant no node_real admission ever named renders UN-AUTHORISED, a superseded stage side an authorised alias; tests.test_field runs 12/12 green here including the fabricated-clean-ladder pair. The Section-10 check that could have failed and did not: this version's central claim is delivery evidence, and v0 proved this exact story can lie about loop/field.py existing - had the file again been absent, rcp.merge.101 unfindable, or the missed receipt misquoted, this would have been a reject; each was checked and held. Honest residue, not blocking: the story's 'suite 572 green' was not re-run in full here (only the field module); the merge-node's land is the standing evidence for the full suite. L0's v0 accept (rcp.460a2559520e) stands as history; this accept is the same value, now with the artifact behind it.
arc epic.the-field confirmed by bdo (adm.6e3ccedc1f0b) — the owner's standing stamp

The claim: the first real Field is on the trunk, measurable - loop/field.py, a pure read-only fold producing the arc->epic->story->task->environment->node->occupant ladder with evidence ids and next_safe_move, gaps surfaced not invented, occupancy carrying authority. Measured against the tree and the record, not the story's say-so: loop/field.py exists on main and writes nothing (read folds and stdout only); tests/test_field.py runs green (12 tests, including the SS10 fabrication checks: task/environment surface as named gaps with no evidence, an unadmitted writer renders UN-AUTHORISED, a superseded stage side reads as authorised alias); python -m loop.field --arc epic.the-field runs live and emits the full 7-rung ladder with record-id evidence (adm.6e3ccedc1f0b on the arc rung, receipt ids on the story rung, seating admissions on node/occupant) and surfaces 8 gaps honestly; the landing rcp.merge.101 is on receipts.jsonl, written by merge-node.claude.v1 under bdo's arc confirmation adm.6e3ccedc1f0b. The SS10 evidence that could have been absent and was not: loop/field.py itself - on v0 this exact artifact did not exist and this gate returned missed (rcp.3e08d1446381); v1's claim is true precisely because the file, its tests, and its merge receipt are now on the record. This confirm closes the piece only: it does NOT complete epic.the-field - five pieces (atom.signal-layer.v0, atom.field-portal.v0, atom.living-flow.v0, atom.projective-links.v0, atom.session-field.v0) are absent on the epic rung.
TimeoutError: timed out

Owner value on the record: bdo named this exact hole -- 'it should be impossible to create work in GitHub without going through the loop, and it is not' (cited in loop/pr_audit.py docstring; retro 0037, why_now). The atom invariant (every work-particle is an atom on the log, S15/D-5) lived only client-side (the PR pen's atom_backed_refusal, the lander); a branch pushed straight to a PR met neither and was invisible to every fold ambient control runs (PR #107 the proof). This closes that seam: loop.pr_audit.orphan_reason is a pure fold the server-side atom-invariant.yml runs via the SAME pr.py audit verb the pen uses, so client and server can never drift on what 'on the log' means, and it enforces toward the log only -- surfaces orphans, never judges the work (D-2). The story tells this value fully (correct seam author-to-value, handoff to owner-stamp, serves epic.substrate), so not amend. S10 check that could have failed and did not: whether this value is the owner's or merely the agent's -- a 'make it impossible to route around the loop' gate could read as agent-convenience self-governance, which would be reject_no_value; it is not, because bdo asked for it on the record. Confirmed I did not author this atom (authored by value-loop.story-author.v0, evt.5661a3d1fd5c), no prior receipts on this version.

Owner-value, not agent-convenience: the story closes the envoy's missing return leg so a foreign reviewer's response becomes value-gated, queued work under one proposed arc (one confirm-arc, not N stamps) instead of stranding in read-only docs/sources or report prose the queue never sees — retro 0037 one layer up, the loop running beside the work. That is the atom-invariant bdo stamped (every work-particle walks the gates), and the second-set-of-eyes/disclosure surface he steers, not a story that merely unblocks me. Right seam and scale: one atom backing done-line 0059 at author-to-value, handing off to value-to-placement. The backing is demonstrated, not vapor — envoy.py respond is built and tests/test_envoy.py is green (21 tests) with real §10 teeth proven: a response to a never-sealed package refuses (cannot receive what was never sent) and a colliding atom refuses (editing an atom restarts its pipeline). §10 check that could have failed and didn't: whether the value is the owner's or the agent's — had this story been 'give me a convenient inbox for reviews' it would be reject_no_value; it is instead the structural hole that lets external review become governed work.

Accept. Value is the owner's and on the record: the story is bdo's own ('As bdo, I want a security MODE...'), and it serves the standing least-permissions direction (bdo, 2026-06-10) by turning a deny-only guard into a scoped observer so bdo can see the SHAPE of what the fence blocks without un-guarding. Real, non-trivial value at the right seam/scale (epic.substrate, author-to-value). Because this touches CORE/SECURITY files I verified the fail-safe claim myself and it HOLDS: (1) reconcile.active_mode(fold,session) initializes posture='normal' and only flips on a positive enabled security_mode admission; enabled:false closes back to normal; any exception returns ('normal',None) — strict default, never silently un-guarded. (2) command_guard.read_mode fails open to ('normal',None) and logs 'degraded'; in hook() a deny with training=False takes the unchanged exit-2 firm-line path, so with NO admission the guard denies EXACTLY as before — relaxation (would-deny+exit0) happens ONLY when a bdo-signed train admission is active for the calling session. (3) node.mode_train refuses any signer != bdo (writes nothing, exit 2) — no self-granted un-guard, D-4. (4) tests/test_train_mode.py passes 4/4 incl. the §10 negative control: git add . denied exit2 with no admission, same bytes exit0 would-deny under active train, divergence asserted (2,0); plus a session-signer-refused test and an unreadable-fold-stays-normal test. The one check that could have failed and did not (§10): the negative control — if the default posture were not strict, or read_mode failed open to train, git add . would have returned 0 with no admission and the whole change would be a default-guard-weakening regression = NEGATIVE value; it returns 2. Additive, fail-safe, default security untouched.

The check that could have failed and didn't: the cut can only ever HOLD more branches, never delete more -- it adds a gate before delete, relaxes nothing, and is inert until bdo stamps the policy. Value confirmed.

Verified the work is real, not vapor: causality/story.html (254-line new file on this branch vs origin/main) genuinely implements all four ANIM tokens canvas-system.js declared idle. canvas-system.js lines 73-80 declare six motions (wobble, water-fill, pulse-travel, ring, membrane-breath, entrance-fade); story.html renders the four the prior renderer never used — waterDisc() (water-fill, level folded from glyph degree in the mesh), pulseDot() travelling each edge (pulse-travel, rust on feedback loops), an activation ring on open (ringT), and staggered entrance scale+fade (birthA). The felt field is folded from structure (energy from edge density, tempo from loop count), not painted, honoring the anima 'fold not paint' line. Value is the owner's, on the record: bdo asked for animations and polish and confirmed direction on live screenshots, recorded as iterations.md 0009; the atom serves epic.causality-surface, confirmed by bdo (adm.a675cb9d36fb), whose charter is exactly 'a place to be seen and steered' — this is the closing increment that lands the whole hero surface to the served URL. No prior receipts on this atom version, no hesitation to inherit. The §10 check that could have failed and didn't: the four 'idle' tokens could have stayed claimed-but-unwired (a story.html that narrated motion without rendering it, or a stub file) — I read the actual draw loop and each of the four is genuinely wired into rendering, so the accept could conceivably have been a reject_no_value had the file been vapor.

Diffed causality/story.html against origin/main (70 insertions) and confirmed all four claims are implemented, not narrated: (a) a real ICONS Set of eight names plus a drawIcon(name,x,y,u,col) library of fine-marker ink line-art, drawn in the glyph via if(g.icon && ICONS.has(g.icon)) with the word shrunk beneath, abstract glyphs staying word-only; (b) the edge label now drawn on a cx.roundRect paper chip in a bolder Caveat hand and gated on edge length (if(label && d>62)) so it is no longer buried on the dashed line and suppressed on short edges; (c) a @media(max-width:620px) block that repositions .phrases/.tools/.zoom/.lens/.top so the chrome stops colliding at small widths; (d) a per-frame VIEW{s,tx,ty} fit-to-viewport transform (translate+scale, capped at 1) wrapping all graph drawing with a matching cx.restore(), the sentence skin shrunk to fit width (while(total>maxW && fs>9)), and the pointer hit-test inverse-transformed ((sx-VIEW.tx)/VIEW.s) so clicks still land. The value is the owner's, on the record: bdo named this working mode directly (iterate on the small visual expectations that keep being assumed away, and work at small sizes), and it serves the bdo-confirmed epic.causality-surface. Cost of a wrong call is low and reversible (experience layer only, static Pages artifacts, nothing written back). The check that could have failed and didnt: the pointer hit-test could have stayed in raw screen space after the VIEW transform was added, silently breaking every click/hover under scaling; the diff shows pos() actually inverse-transforms by VIEW, so the responsive fit did not cost the interaction.
